mechanism_narrative: Sustained expansion in industrial production signals improving aggregate supply conditions and corporate earnings growth, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rolling 2-year Rank IC of INDPRO change vs 60-day forward return is below 0.03 or negative for any consecutive 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment rate drops below 4.0%, an extremely tight labor market forces aggressive wage growth that erodes corporate margins and triggers Fed tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns within the next week.
failure_condition: In instances where UNRATE < 4.0%, the 5-day forward S&P 500 return is positive more than 50% of the time over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous high inflation and rising policy rates indicate the Fed is actively tightening into an overheating economy, which compresses valuation multiples and depresses S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year > 2.5% and FEDFUNDS increasing over prior 3 months
failure_condition: The joint signal of high CPI and rising FEDFUNDS produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 40% of triggered instances over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts late in the economic expansion foreshadow a demand pullback and recession, leading to negative S&P 500 returns. This signal is only meaningful when the economy is already in late-cycle with tight labor and high capacity utilization.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month moving average below its 12-month moving average
failure_condition: The rolling 3-year Sharpe ratio of a portfolio that shorts SPX on this signal is less than 0.2 or the signal fails to predict negative returns in >60% of out-of-sample instances.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated retail and food services sales indicate robust consumer spending, which directly supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a short weekly horizon as market participants price in the momentum.
failure_condition: The monthly change in RSAFS produces a Rank IC below 0.04 for 5-day forward returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate decisively above 2% signals the Fed has entered or is deep into a tightening cycle that raises discount rates and reduces equity risk appetite, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 2.0%, the average 60-day forward S&P 500 return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10) over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strong payroll growth (above 200k) and very low unemployment (below 4.5%) indicate a fully overheated labor market that forces labor cost increases and triggers Fed tightening, creating headwinds for S&P 500 returns in the next month.
failure_condition: The joint condition yields negative 20-day forward returns less than 60% of the time, or the average return is not significantly below the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth accelerates strongly (above 2.5% quarter-over-quarter annualized) during the early phase of a recovery from recession, the economy has ample slack and the momentum translates into above-average S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
regime_hint: post-recession recovery (GDPC1 growth positive after negative quarter)
failure_condition: In post-recession periods with GDPC1 growth > 2.5%, the 60-day forward S&P 500 return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or occurs in less than 70% of such instances.
